key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn how local fiber orientation, clustering, and porosity control the static nonlinear response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics.
engineering_relevance: It enables fast, physics-informed screening of weak microstructural regions and supports digital-twin workflows for lightweight polymer composite parts.
method: Reconstruct microstructures from micro-CT and discretize them into Voronoi-based interaction cells.
Run nonlinear FE homogenization on each cell with matrix damage to generate stress-strain histories.
Train a GNN-LSTM to encode microstructural topology plus response evolution over load history.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches unseen FE stress-strain responses with about R² ≈ 0.98 while reducing computation by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance depends on representative training microstructures and experimentally calibrated damage laws, so extrapolation to very different processing conditions may be limited.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
